events.title: Introduction to Complementary Food & Making Your Own Baby Food
events.description: In this workshop, participants will learn about the introduction to complementary food and healthy eating for children ages 6 to 12 months. We will also cover the importance and benefits of breastfeeding, introducing iron rich foods as the first food, other food sources and concepts and teaching on how to prepare and make healthy baby food.

events.title: Screen Time with Toronto Public Health
events.description: In this session, the benefits and impacts of screen time will be explored. Discussion will include looking at recommendations for screen time per age of the child (between 0 and 6 years of age) and how to model and manage appropriate screen time behaviour. Physical activity recommendations as an alternative to screen time will be reviewed.

events.title: Pokémon Club
events.description: Whether you're a new or expert Pokémon Trainer, everyone is welcome! Show off your Pokémon knowledge and research skills and join our exciting Pokémon scavenger hunt for free Pokémon cards! Feel free to bring in your old Pokémon cards to play with or trade with staff and other Pokémon trainers!

events.title: Game on! Board Game Playtime
events.description: Join us for a fun-filled day of board games and puzzles! Play your favourites or try something new! Brings your friends and family or meet new people at the library. Games available for use in the Children's Area. Available games include: Ball Fall Jumbo (Ages 3+), STEM Pretend Flower Garden Toy Set (Ages 3-6), Operation (Ages 6+), STEM Road Builder Game (Ages 4-8), Dinosaur Tetra Tower Balance Stacking Blocks Game (Ages 6+) Available puzzles include: Giant World Map Puzzle (

events.title: Crafturday
events.description: Join us for a day of fun and crafts. Partake in a new and engaging craft every Saturday from 9:30am - 4:30pm. Drop-in and self-serve. For pre-school and school-aged children and their caregivers. If your child lives with a disability, and requires an accessibility accommodation to participate in this program, please contact Accessibility Services at accessibleservices@tpl.ca or phone 416-393-7099. Please contact us at least three weeks in advance.

events.description: Do you like to build with LEGO, DUPLO, Feltro, Snap Circuits or KEVA? Join us every Saturday morning for some fun building challenges! All children welcome with their caregivers. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: The Dr. Bonkers Show
events.description: Join Dr. Bonkers as the colourful entertainer brings joy to kids and families! With music and instruments, Dr. Bonkers and his best puppet friend, Larry, teach lessons about friendship, kindness, and imagination. events.title: Scientists in School: Science Meets Art
events.description: Discover what happens when science meets art! Explore perception as you use shadows, colour, motion, and light to create art from science. You’ll make a puppet, a spinning top and a kaleidoscope, as well as design your own galaxy. “The greatest scientists are always artists as well!” (Albert Einstein) Please note: this program is suitable for children 4 years old and above with adult accompaniment. events.title: Pawsitively Pets: Hands-on Animal Education
events.description: Pawsitively Pets is a hands-on animal education program for children of all ages. Our wonderful animal team is made up of an assortment of rescued pets that have found their forever home with us. Our goal is to provide our animal team with a permanent home and the best possible care, while sharing our love or animals with others. All programs focus on responsible pet ownership, and the life-long commitment of owning a pet. Please note this program is suitable for children 5 years old up with adu

events.title: Toddler Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs and rhymes in this program for children, 19 months to 3 years, and their parents or caregivers. Toddler Time runs from 10 a.m. to 10:30 a.m.

events.title: L'Heure du conte / French Storytime
events.description: Rejoignez-nous pour des histoires, des chansons et des comptines en français pour les enfants âgés de 0 à 5 ans, accompagnés de leurs parents et responsables. Les familles sont invitées à rester et à jouer pour 30 minutes supplémentaires après la conclusion du programme. Enjoy stories, songs and rhymes in French at a staff-led story time for children age 0-5 with their parents and caregivers. Families are welcome to "stay and play" for an additional 30 minutes following the program. -------

events.title: EarlyON Family Resource Program
events.description: Learn about quality community resources for young families, enjoy a circle time for the young ones, and get some nutritious snacks. Suitable for families with children birth to six years old.  In partnership with EarlyON Child and Family Centre of Regent Park Community Health Centre.

events.title: Baby Time
events.description: Bouncing and tickling rhymes, songs and stories for babies from birth to 18 months with their parents or caregivers. Baby Time runs from 10:30 to 11:00am. Babies, parents and caregivers are invited to Stay and Play until 11:30am.

events.title: Together Time
events.description: This is a program for parents and caregivers with children from birth - 6 years. Families have the opportunity to socialize while children develop new skills in an early learning environment.

events.title: Lego Club
events.description: Every Wednesday come out to build, create and use your imagination with LEGO. Enjoy a new building challenge each week. Complete 5 challenges and get a sticker! Drop-in.  March Break Challenge: Build a mall as a group from 2-3pm If you identify as a person with a disability and require an accessibility accommodation to participate in this program, please contact Accessibility Services by email, accessibleservices@tpl.ca, or voicemail, 416-393-7099, to make a request. events.title: Blocks Challenges
events.description: Join us on a Saturday morning for a building activity! Complete the challenges using Mega Bloks and Lego Duplo blocks. events.title: Unconventional Materials: An Osborne Collection Exhibit
events.description: An exhibition of intricately crafted books, artworks and much more. The Unconventional Materials exhibit highlights the use of unconventional techniques and materials in children’s literature, featuring historical and modern examples.   Visit the Osborne Collection to experience our exhibit featuring items such as plasticine art by Barbara Reid, dioramas and panoramas, postcards and other ephemera, and hornbooks and battledores. Located on the 4th floor of the Lillian H. Smith Branch. Free.

events.title: Scavenger Hunt Tuesdays
events.description: Join us anytime of the day every Tuesday of the month; try your best to find all of the items hidden around the library’s children section each Tuesday. Find them all and win a fun prize!  Drop in, no registration required. events.title: Discovery Club: Podcasting
events.description: Have you ever wanted to share your stories, ideas, or favorite topics with the world? In this workshop series, you’ll work in small groups to create your very own short podcast from brainstorming your show idea to recording and editing your first episode! Each week, we’ll explore a new step in the podcasting process. This program is for ages 9 to 12 years old.

events.title: Therapy Dogs
events.description: Join us for small group visits with a therapy dog! Children will have an opportunity to read, listen to stories and engage in therapeutic human-animal interaction. This free program is presented in partnership with One Health Partners. Suitable for school-aged children aged 5-12. Please Note: A maximum of 5 children can participate in the program during each 15-minute time slot. Timeslots are available on a first come first serve basis.

events.title: Robotic Explorers Club
events.description: For ages 8-12. Think you can command a robot? It’s harder than it looks! Join us for a hands-on session where you are the programmer. We’ll be using block coding to program robots to win games and complete challenges. Bring your critical thinking skills and be prepared for plenty of independent problem-solving! events.title: Mad Science Workshop - Bugs!
events.description: Explore the world of arthropods! Children examine insect anatomy up close and classify creepy crawlers into insects and non-insects. events.title: Ewww...that Science is Gross!
events.description: Discover the science behind things that make you go...ewww! Make "snot" and sneeze it while exploring viscosity, identify various animals from their scat (i.e. poop) and learn about digestion while producing a burp in a bag. Join us on a gross but fun-filled science adventure!

events.description: How well can you read handwritten items? This free guided tour of Loops, Swoops and Curlicues: A legacy of handwritten history  will lead your through hundreds of years of handwritten items from TPL Special Collections.  Come test your decoding skills as we explore several hundred years of handwritten words. events.title: Youth Hub: DIY Puzzles
events.description: We will provide the blank puzzles, and you will provide the imagination as you create your own custom artwork for a jigsaw puzzle. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Board Game Café
events.description: Chill and learn to play various kinds of board games. There are single player board games to multi-player games to choose from. This is a drop-in program.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.title: Pride Fuse Bead Keychains
events.description: Celebrate Pride Month at Toronto Public Library by creating a Pride themed Fuse Bead Keychain to hang on your backpack or your jeans! events.title: Container Gardening: Supporting Our Local Pollinators in Small Spaces
events.description: Pollinators need a supply of nectar throughout the growing season, so if you are planting up pots for your garden or balcony, why not choose plants that will attract birds, bees, and butterflies?  This demonstration, led by a Toronto Master Gardener, will start with how to choose soil, plants, and containers, and how to arrange them to maximize their positive impact on the environment. events.title: Bars & Beats
events.description: Coach Desiree McKenzie guides participants through an exploration of rap music as poetry in motion. Participants will analyze lyrics and create their own original spoken word pieces inspired by musical flow and technique.

events.title: Teen Quiet Study
events.description: Are you looking for a quiet space to study after school? Join our quiet study hall at Locke's program room every Thursday for individual or group study. events.title: Throwback Thursdays: Great Movies on the Big Screen
events.description: Come join us for a radical retro rewind projected on the big screen. Whether you're a first-time viewer or long-time fan, these movies are sure to please. March 19: Michael Clayton (2007, 14A)  March 26: Ocean’s Twelve (2004, PG) April 2: Bridesmaids (2011, 14A) April 9: Austin Powers: International Man of Mystery (1997, PG-13) April 16: Meet the Parents (2000, PG) April 23: Rounders (1998, R) April 30: No Country for Old Men (2007, 18A) All are welcome. Drop-in, no registration r

events.title: The Future of AI in Entertainment with James Rowan
events.description: Generative AI is rapidly reshaping how creative work is imagined, produced, and shared. From image and video generation to writing, design, and virtual production, these tools are expanding what's possible while also raising important questions about authorship, labour, and creative identity. Enjoy mindful colouring sheets, rainbow scratch art, mindfulness card decks, gratitude journals, and more. All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by

events.title: Youth Hub: Board Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Tuesday to play board games with your friends! Ask us for our list of board games, and check our monthly calendar for Tournaments with prizes! All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Study Hall Wednesdays
events.description: On Wednesdays, the Eatonville Youth Hub becomes a quiet space for teens to study, work on projects, and request homework help and resume help from staff. Bring your homework and enjoy a snack in a space that is just for teens. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Chess Club
events.description: Drop in for a casual game of chess in a welcoming environment. Improve your chess skills or learn how to play. Sets are provided. All ages. Drop-in.

events.title: Seniors E-Connect
events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have a phone, tablet or computer you don't know how to use or want to learn to use better? Do you enjoy meeting new people? Seniors E-Connect will help you learn tech topics in a fun group setting, but with the one-on-one help of a teen volunteer.

events.title: Book Club
events.description: Come and join us on the third Thursday of every other month to discuss a variety of books. Everyone is welcome! Drop in, no registration required. This year we are reading:  February 19 -  The Strangers by Katherena Vermette April 16 -  Big Little Lies by Liane Moriarty June 18 -  On the Ravine by Vincent Lam September 17 -  Tomorrow, and Tomorrow, and Tomorrow by Gabrielle Zevin November 19 -  Small Things Like These by
